title: CLAUDE.md Monitoring Pipeline table accuracy correction (schedule rot + missing prune-integrity entry + agent count)
author: tinker (Mode 3 audit, run tinker-2026-05-25T06-55)
source_finding: FND-01-tinker-2026-05-25T06-55 (expanded scope of FND-06 from 2026-05-25T02-48 and FND-03 from 2026-05-25T06-08, which were narrowly about analyst-baby cadence only — this audit found the rot is broader)
summary: The Monitoring Pipeline table in CLAUDE.md (lines 155-172) is materially out of sync with the actual scheduled-task crons. Five of twelve listed agents have inaccurate schedule descriptions; one agent (dome-prune-integrity) is missing from the table entirely; the prose count 'Eleven scheduled agents' is wrong (currently 13 task definitions: 11 enabled + 2 DISABLED sloppytoppy variants, of which 12 are listed in the table); and two listed agents (dome-sloppytoppy-score, dome-sloppytoppy-rewrite) are DISABLED per CLAUDE.md's own narrative (workspace-sync disaster section) but appear without that marker. Every new session and every agent reads this table; the inaccuracy compounds across all agents.

evidence.source_of_truth: list_scheduled_tasks tool output (captured at run tinker-2026-05-25T06-55)

discrepancies.actual_description: 4-hour cadence (BAU restored 2026-05-18 AM after weekend burndown). Offset :50 maintains analyst → decider(:10, +80min) → curmudgeon(:30, +80min) routing flow. Hours 0/4/8/12/16/20.
discrepancies.fix: Every 4h (`50 0,4,8,12,16,20 * * *`); BAU; can bump to 30m under load

discrepancies.actual_description: 12-hour cadence (BAU restored 2026-05-18 AM — 4:20 AM + 4:20 PM local, off-peak). Mode 1 BAU tracker drain ONLY. Bump back to 1h or 4h if assigned-analyst grows past 5 again.
discrepancies.fix: Twice daily (`20 4,16 * * *`); 04:20 and 16:20 local; bump to 1h/4h if assigned-analyst > 5

discrepancies.actual_description: 4-hour cadence (BAU restored 2026-05-18 AM after weekend burndown). Offset :30 sits +80min after decider (:10). Hours 3/7/11/15/19/23.
discrepancies.fix: Every 4h (`30 3,7,11,15,19,23 * * *`); BAU; can bump to 30m under load

discrepancies.actual_description: 4-hour cadence (BAU restored 2026-05-18 AM after weekend burndown). Offset :10 sits +80min after analyst (:50). Hours 2/6/10/14/18/22.
discrepancies.fix: Every 4h (`10 2,6,10,14,18,22 * * *`); BAU; can bump to 1h under load

discrepancies.actual_description: PROP-051 Workstream C — daily prune of monitor/integrity/ retention windows. Archives older per-run artifacts to JSONL and deletes sources. Created 2026-05-23 after workspace-sync mass-delete disaster.
discrepancies.fix: Add a new row: | dome-prune-integrity | Daily 09:00 (`0 9 * * *`) | Haiku | (no prompt file — script-only via SKILL.md) | PROP-051 Workstream C — archive monitor/integrity/ per-run artifacts to JSONL on retention windows |

discrepancies.actual_state: enabled=false (DISABLED). Per CLAUDE.md narrative line 110-ish (workspace-sync disaster section): 'dome-sloppytoppy-score + dome-sloppytoppy-rewrite remain DISABLED pending operator decision.' The narrative and the table are inconsistent.
discrepancies.fix: Append ' (DISABLED — pending operator decision)' to the schedule column. OR leave in the table with a footnote referencing the disaster-recovery section.

agent_count_prose.fix: Update to a self-consistent count. Recommendation: 'Twelve scheduled agents are listed below (eleven currently enabled; two DISABLED — see workspace-sync disaster section).' OR add prune-integrity to the table (per fix above) and change to 'Thirteen scheduled agents are listed below (twelve currently enabled; one DISABLED).' OR compute on first read: 'See the table below; current enabled count is N (query via list_scheduled_tasks).' The first option is simplest given how rarely the table changes.

why_this_matters: CLAUDE.md auto-loads into every new Cowork session. Wrong schedule descriptions mislead any agent reasoning about cadence (e.g., 'is X stale? expected every 30m...' → 'no, expected every 4h, it's fine').
Missing prune-integrity entry means any agent doing a 'is the integrity directory growing unbounded?' check won't know there's a pruner.
Disabled sloppytoppy entries shown as active misleads anyone wondering why no recent score-summary.txt updates (FYI: 100h-202h stale, but DISABLED is the expected state — not a finding).
Tinker has itself misread these (Mode 1 freshness check uses '2x its schedule' as the staleness threshold; baseline schedules need to be right).

operations.find: Eleven scheduled agents run continuously. All prompts live in `monitor/prompts/*.md` — edit the markdown to change agent behavior.
operations.replace_with: Twelve scheduled agents handle the monitoring pipeline (ten currently enabled + dome-prune-integrity + workspace-sync, total eleven enabled; two DISABLED — see workspace-sync disaster section under 2026-05-21). All prompts live in `monitor/prompts/*.md` — edit the markdown to change agent behavior. The current enabled count can be queried at any time via the `list_scheduled_tasks` tool.

operations.find: the existing 12-row | Agent | Schedule | Model | Prompt File | Purpose | table at lines 159-172

proposed_change.estimated_diff_size: ~18-line replacement block (similar shape to existing table). Pure documentation edit, no code or data file touched.

implementation_path.option_a_operator_manual: Operator edits CLAUDE.md in a clone, commits, pushes. Simplest path. CLAUDE.md is git-owned so FUSE edit won't reach agents; clone-push is required.
implementation_path.option_b_decider_self_apply: Decider's self-apply flow already handles CLAUDE.md edits (it touched lines from prior runs). Operator can route this PROP as a 'doc patch' through the normal decider intake — but that requires creating an issue to wrap it, which is overkill for a 18-line table replacement.
implementation_path.option_c_tinker_direct: Tinker is empowered for 'Conservative self-fixes — stale URLs, wrong paths, missing fields.' This qualifies, but tinker has not previously pushed CLAUDE.md changes from a clone in any recent run. Adding that capability mid-run is risky on this scheduled invocation (the 2026-05-21 workspace-sync disaster taught the same lesson: agent improvisation under disk/auth pressure compounds). Operator-applied is safer.
implementation_path.recommendation: Option A. Operator can apply this in ~2 minutes via `git -C /tmp/edit-clone` followed by the standard publish flow. PROP serves as the spec for what to change.

verification_pattern: grep -q 'Twelve scheduled agents handle the monitoring pipeline' CLAUDE.md && grep -q 'dome-prune-integrity' CLAUDE.md && grep -q 'Twice daily (`20 4,16' CLAUDE.md && grep -q 'Daily 03:30 (`30 3 \* \* \*`)' CLAUDE.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
blast_radius: Documentation only. No code change, no script change, no schema change, no data file touched. CLAUDE.md is loaded by every session/agent on read so the fix propagates immediately. No agent decision logic actually keys off the exact prose strings; the inaccuracy is informational, not behavioral.
rejection_criteria: If the operator plans to re-enable sloppytoppy soon (within days), the DISABLED markers in the table will become stale immediately — better to defer this PROP until that decision lands. (Mitigation: keep the markers but link to the disaster-recovery section that explains the gating decision.)

If schedule descriptions in CLAUDE.md are deliberately fuzzy ('Variable') to give the operator latitude to swap between churn-and-burn and BAU without touching docs, then this PROP over-specifies. (Counter-argument: the current text mixes specificity styles — some rows have cron expressions, some say 'Variable' — and the actual cron is the ground truth either way; the table should match it.)
linked_findings: FND-03-tinker-2026-05-25T06-08 (narrow scope, analyst-baby only — superseded by this PROP)
FND-06-tinker-2026-05-25T02-48 (narrow scope, analyst-baby only — superseded by this PROP)
operator_action_required: 1. Apply the operations above to CLAUDE.md in a clone.
2. Commit + push via the standard clone path (`git -C /tmp/edit-clone commit -am 'PROP-057: CLAUDE.md monitoring pipeline schedule accuracy fix'` then `git push`).
3. workspace-sync's git→FUSE direction (which fires on every publish or hourly) will then propagate to FUSE; agents read updated CLAUDE.md on next invocation.
4. Optional: leave this PROP as `proposed`. The next tinker run will see verification_pattern returns FIXED and update PROP-057 status to `implemented` per the lifecycle rule.
